## Summary
Big Bang Nucleosynthesis (BBN) predictions for light element abundances (D, ³He, ⁴He, ⁷Li) provide a critical test of the standard cosmological model at T ~ 1 MeV. Deuterium and helium-4 abundances are in excellent agreement with CMB-inferred baryon density.

## Evidence
Observed primordial deuterium D/H = (2.547 ± 0.025) × 10⁻⁵ from quasar absorption systems matches BBN predictions using Planck's Ωbh² = 0.02237. Helium-4 mass fraction Yp = 0.2449 ± 0.0040 is consistent. The lithium-7 problem (factor ~3 discrepancy) remains unresolved.

## Significance
BBN concordance validates the hot Big Bang model back to ~1 second. The lithium problem may indicate new physics (e.g., particle decays) or systematic errors in stellar lithium measurements.
